title: cellpaintr Vignette
text: High-content imaging can capture detailed morphological functional features single-cell level. widely used drug screen assays generating large-scale image datasets machine learning models. Software CellProfiler extract features high-content images downstream analysis. CellProfiler implements suite traditional image processing algorithms segment cells extract 1,000 morphological texture features. simplify data analysis CellProfiler features R—many options exist Python, PyCytominer—developed R/Bioconductor package. package leverages Bioconductor objects batch correction dimension reduction, lowering learning curve users familiar single-cell RNA sequencing workflows.

title: cellpaintr Vignette
code: if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {     install.packages("BiocManager") } BiocManager::install("cellpaintr")

title: cellpaintr Vignette
code: library(cellpaintr) library(scater) library(purrr) library(dplyr) library(ggrepel)

title: cellpaintr Vignette
text: Load CellProfiler output create SingleCellExperiment object.
code: set.seed(23) cell_file <- generate_data() sce <- loadData(cell_file)

title: cellpaintr Vignette
text: Prepare data machine learning.  Transform non-negatives features scale features.
code: # remove cells with missing features sce <- removeNAs(sce)  # number of cells plotCellsPerImage(sce) # remove outlier images sce <- removeOutliers(sce, min = 0, max = 300)  # remove outlier cells stats <- perCellQCMetrics(sce, assay.type = "features") sce <- sce[, !isOutlier(stats$sum)]  # remove features with zero spread sce <- removeLowVariance(sce, robust = TRUE)  # remove zero-inflated features sce <- removeZeroInflation(sce) sce <- transformLogScale(sce, robust = TRUE)

title: cellpaintr Vignette
text: Pseudo-bulk images. Use scater exploratory data analysis. PCA pseudo-bulk features.   driving PC1?  UMAP.
code: sce_aggr <- aggregateAcrossCells(     sce,     id = colData(sce)[, c("ImageNumber", "Drug", "Patient")],     use.assay.type = "tfmfeatures",     statistics = "mean" ) sce_aggr <- scater::runPCA(sce_aggr,     exprs_values = "tfmfeatures",     ncomponents = 10 ) plotReducedDim(sce_aggr, dimred = "PCA", colour_by = "Patient") plotReducedDim(sce_aggr, dimred = "PCA", colour_by = "Drug") plotPCACor(sce_aggr, filter_by = 1) sce_aggr <- runUMAP(sce_aggr, exprs_values = "tfmfeatures") plotUMAP(sce_aggr, colour_by = "Patient") plotUMAP(sce_aggr, colour_by = "Drug")

title: cellpaintr Vignette
text: Compare positive control negative control. Summarize feature subgroups volcano plot.  Individual scores.    Compare list drugs negative control. Custom volcano plot summarizing drugs faceted features.
code: set.seed(23)  sce$Drug <- as.factor(sce$Drug) sce$Drug <- relevel(sce$Drug, ref = "D1")  types <- c("AreaShape", "Intensity", "Texture")  sce_single <- predictLOO(     sce,     target = "Drug", group = "Patient",     interest_level = "D7", reference_level = "D1",     types = types,     n_threads = 1 ) volcanoPlot(sce_single,     target = "Drug", group = "Patient",     p_cutoff = 0.05, fc_cutoff = 0.5 ) plotLOO(sce_single, target = "Drug", group = "Patient") plotAUC(sce_single, target = "Drug", group = "Patient") plotROC(sce_single, target = "Drug", group = "Patient") set.seed(23)  reference_level <- "D1" interest_levels <- setdiff(levels(sce$Drug), reference_level)  sce_list <- map(interest_levels, function(interest_level) {     predictLOO(         sce,         target = "Drug", group = "Patient",         interest_level = interest_level, reference_level = reference_level,         types = types,         n_threads = 1     ) }, .progress = "drug screening") # prepare stats table p_adj_cutoff <- 0.05 fc_cutoff <- 0.5 tb_stats <- lapply(sce_list, calculateStats,     target = "Drug", group = "Patient" ) |>     bind_rows() |>     mutate(pvalue_adj = p.adjust(pvalue, method = "BH")) |>     mutate(Selection = ifelse(         pvalue_adj < p_adj_cutoff & log2FoldChange > fc_cutoff,         Target, ""     ))  # volcano plot tb_stats |>     ggplot(aes(log2FoldChange, -log10(pvalue_adj), label = Selection)) +     geom_vline(xintercept = c(0, fc_cutoff), alpha = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +     geom_hline(         yintercept = c(0, -log10(p_adj_cutoff)), alpha = 0.5,         linetype = "dashed"     ) +     geom_point(aes(color = Target)) +     geom_text_repel(aes(color = Target), max.overlaps = Inf) +     xlab("log2 fold change") +     ylab("-log10 p-value (BH adjusted)") +     facet_wrap(~Feature) +     ggtitle(paste0("False Discovery Rate: ", 100 * p_adj_cutoff, "%"))

title: Plot predicted leave-one-out probabilities — volcanoPlot
code: set.seed(23) cell_file <- generate_data() sce <- loadData(cell_file) sce <- transformLogScale(sce)  sce$Drug <- as.factor(sce$Drug) sce$Drug <- relevel(sce$Drug, ref = "D1") types <- c("AreaShape", "Intensity", "Texture")  sce_single <- predictLOO(     sce,     target = "Drug", group = "Patient",     interest_level = "D7", reference_level = "D1",     types = types,     n_threads = 1 )  volcanoPlot(sce_single,     target = "Drug", group = "Patient",     p_cutoff = 0.05, fc_cutoff = 0.5 )
